# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.

Write secure code.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-a-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-a
  port:
    number: 8080
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-a-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: service-a-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: service-b
          namespace: default
        - name: service-c
          namespace: default

---

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-b-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-b
  port:
    number: 9090
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-b-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: service-b-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: service-a
          namespace: default

---

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-c-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-c
  port:
    number: 7070
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-c-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: service-c-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: service-a
          namespace: default
